mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are negative in >55% of months following a rate cut, or rolling Rank IC < 0.02 over 3 years.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in >60% of triggered months, or the signal fails to separate bull/bear outcomes with p < 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates broad-based domestic demand growth, which drives corporate earnings revisions and produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in RSAFS and HOUST yields negative 60-day returns in >50% of cases, or interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in the index of industrial production during a late-cycle expansion phase reflects capacity constraints and slowing output, which triggers profit margin compression and negative S&P 500 returns over the following 20 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during late-cycle phases are followed by positive 20-day returns in >65% of instances, or the predictive power vanishes when GDPC1 growth exceeds 3.0% annualized.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in total nonfarm payrolls reflect strengthening economic activity and higher wage growth, which supports consumer spending and generates positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive payroll surprises yield negative 5-day returns in >55% of months, or the signal's Rank IC remains below 0.01 across all rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A 12-month inflation rate exceeding 2.5% signals overheating demand and forces the Federal Reserve to accelerate rate hikes, which increases discount rates and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Inflation readings above 2.5% are followed by positive 60-day returns in >60% of cases, or the threshold effect disappears when controlling for GDP growth.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent quarterly growth in real GDP and real personal consumption expenditures confirms synchronized demand expansion, which improves corporate revenue forecasts and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint positive growth signal yields negative 20-day returns in >55% of quarters, or the interaction term loses statistical significance (p > 0.05) in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the index of industrial production during a post-shock recovery phase signals restoring manufacturing capacity and supply chain normalization, which reduces input cost pressures and produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases in recovery phases are followed by negative 5-day returns in >60% of months, or the predictive coefficient flips sign when UNRATE exceeds 5.0%.
